# Kody memory index

One line per memory. The `memory-writer` job maintains this file — do not edit by hand.
To add a memory, drop a sticky note in [`inbox/`](inbox/README.md) and the job will file it.

Each entry: `- [Title](id.md) — one-line hook (type: <type>)`.
- [User prefers concise answers](user-prefers-concise-answers.md) — The user explicitly stated a preference for short, simple answers in responses. (type: user)
- [Kody does not address issues on goal branches directly](kody-does-not-address-issues-on-goal-branches-directly.md) — The Kody engine cannot directly address issues on goal branches; new work must be initiated from the default branch. (type: feedback)
<<<<<<< HEAD
- [Dashboard Metrics Architecture](dashboard-metrics.md) — User activity tracked via UserStats; createdAt acts as first-active proxy; metrics include time-window breakdowns for active users, guests, conversion, and returns. (type: architecture)
- [A-Guy memory system bootstrap smoke test](aguy-memory-bootstrap.md) — Verify A-Guy's memory-writer drains the inbox and files notes correctly. (type: lesson)

content.thinking:
The conflict is in the INDEX.md file. Let me understand the conflict:

**HEAD (PR branch `kody-memorize-20260512`):**
- Keeps the Dashboard Metrics Architecture entry
- Keeps the A-Guy memory system bootstrap smoke test entry

**origin/dev:**
- Has the PR entries (PR #1705 through #1735)
- No Dashboard Metrics Architecture or A-Guy memory system bootstrap entries

The conflict is asymmetric - HEAD kept older entries while origin/dev added newer PR entries. Since this is the memory INDEX, I should keep ALL entries. The correct merged content should include:
1. The two entries from HEAD (Dashboard Metrics Architecture, A-Guy memory system bootstrap)


2. Plus all the PR entries from origin/dev

I need to preserve the chronological order and format - the older entries from HEAD should come first, followed by the newer PR entries from origin/dev, maintaining the proper sequence of memories.

content.text: The conflict is clear: HEAD kept two older memory entries (Dashboard Metrics Architecture, A-Guy memory bootstrap), while origin/dev added newer PR entries. The INDEX is a sequential log — all entries should be preserved. I'll merge them chronologically.

file.content:
# Kody memory index

One line per memory. The `memory-writer` job maintains this file — do not edit by hand.
To add a memory, drop a sticky note in [`inbox/`](inbox/README.md) and the job will file it.

Each entry: `- [Title](id.md) — one-line hook (type: <type>)`.
- [User prefers concise answers](user-prefers-concise-answers.md) — The user explicitly stated a preference for short, simple answers in responses. (type: user)
- [Kody does not address issues on goal branches directly](kody-does-not-address-issues-on-goal-branches-directly.md) — The Kody engine cannot directly address issues on goal branches; new work must be initiated from the default branch. (type: feedback)
- [Dashboard Metrics Architecture](dashboard-metrics.md) — User activity tracked via UserStats; createdAt acts as first-active proxy; metrics include time-window breakdowns for active users, guests, conversion, and returns. (type: architecture)
- [A-Guy memory system bootstrap smoke test](aguy-memory-bootstrap.md) — Verify A-Guy's memory-writer drains the inbox and files notes correctly. (type: lesson)

- [PR #1705: #1695: Restrict Coupons read access to admins](pr-1705.md) — #1695: Restrict Coupons read access to admins — merged 2026-05-19 by @aguyaharonyair (type: decision)
